question: [File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: systemEntity()] Can an unprivileged external user submit crafted configuration input that overrides the `shard` or `realm` values in `CommonProperties`, causing `SystemEntity.toEntityId()` to resolve all system entity IDs (address book 101/102, fee schedule, exchange rate) to wrong shard/realm coordinates, permanently corrupting the mirror node's interpretation of Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: systemEntity()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the `@ConfigurationPropertiesScan('org.hiero.mirror')` annotation to inject a rogue `hiero.mirror.common.shard` or `hiero.mirror.common.realm` property via an externally reachable configuration endpoint, causing `SystemEntity` to map `addressBookFile101()` and `addressBookFile102()` to wrong entity IDs and silently accept a tampered address book as legitimate Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: systemEntity()] Can an unprivileged user trigger a race condition between `CommonProperties.init()` setting the `INSTANCE` singleton and `SystemEntity` lazy-getter evaluation, causing `toEntityId()` to compute entity IDs from a partially-initialized `CommonProperties` with default `shard=0`/`realm=0` even when the node is configured for a different shard, leading to misidentification of critical Hashgraph history files?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: systemEntity()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a request that forces re-evaluation of the `SystemEntity` bean's lazy getters (e.g., `addressBookFile101()`, `networkPropertyFile()`) after a hot-reload of `CommonProperties`, causing the mirror node to use inconsistent entity IDs across concurrent Hashgraph history processing threads and accept tampered records as valid?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: hibernatePropertiesCustomizer()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the fact that `HBM2DDL_AUTO` is set to `'none'` to submit crafted JSON payloads that, when deserialized by `CustomJsonFormatMapper`, insert malformed Hashgraph history records into the database without schema validation catching the inconsistency, permanently corrupting the historical ledger state?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: hibernatePropertiesCustomizer()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the `hibernate.criteria.literal_handling_mode=BIND` setting by crafting bind parameter values that bypass application-level validation and inject tampered consensus timestamps or sequence numbers into Hashgraph history records stored via Hibernate Criteria API queries?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: hibernatePropertiesCustomizer()] Can an unprivileged user abuse the `GENERATE_STATISTICS=true` Hibernate setting to extract timing side-channel information about Hashgraph history query patterns, enabling inference of which consensus records are being actively processed and targeting those records for replay or substitution attacks?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: dataSource()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the `dataSource()` bean's use of `dataSourceProperties.determineUrl()` to inject a crafted JDBC URL via an externally reachable Spring Boot actuator or environment endpoint, redirecting the mirror node's database connection to an attacker-controlled database containing fabricated Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: dataSource()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the `JdbcConnectionDetails` provider override path (lines 79-84) where `connectionDetails.getJdbcUrl()` replaces `dataSourceProperties.determineUrl()`, by injecting a malicious `JdbcConnectionDetails` bean that redirects the data source to a tampered Hashgraph history database?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: dataSource()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the `@ConditionalOnMissingBean(DataSource.class)` condition to register a rogue `DataSource` bean before `CommonConfiguration` initializes, causing the mirror node to use an attacker-controlled data source that serves fabricated Hashgraph history records?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: dataSource()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the `hikariConfig.copyStateTo(config)` call (line 89) where a shared `HikariConfig` bean is copied into a new config, by manipulating `spring.datasource.hikari` properties to set a malicious `connectionInitSql` that modifies session-level database settings and corrupts Hashgraph history reads?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: hikariConfig()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the `@ConfigurationProperties('spring.datasource.hikari')` binding on `hikariConfig()` to inject a `connectionInitSql` property that executes arbitrary SQL on every new connection, enabling silent modification of Hashgraph history data at the database session level?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: dbWaiter()] Can an unprivileged user exploit `DatabaseWaiter.waitForDatabase()` by causing repeated connection failures that exhaust the `timeout` window, forcing the application to throw `IllegalStateException` and restart, creating a denial-of-service window during which Hashgraph history records are not being validated or stored?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: spelHelper()] Can an unprivileged user exploit `SpelHelper.hashCode(Object[])` (line 24) by crafting API inputs that produce hash collisions in SpEL-based cache key expressions, causing the mirror node to return cached Hashgraph history data for a different entity or consensus timestamp, effectively serving tampered history to downstream consumers?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: spelHelper()] Can an unprivileged user exploit `SpelHelper.isNullOrEmpty()` returning `true` for a null collection to bypass a cache population guard, causing the mirror node to skip caching a valid Hashgraph history result and instead re-query a database that has been tampered with between requests?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: systemEntity()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the `entityTransactionExclusionDefault` set (line 58-59) in `SystemEntity` by manipulating `shard`/`realm` so that `feeCollectionAccount()`, `networkAdminFeeAccount()`, `nodeRewardAccount()`, and `stakingRewardAccount()` resolve to wrong entity IDs, causing legitimate system transactions to be excluded from history while attacker-injected transactions are included?
